title: LLui consumer migration plan
description: The structured artifact a Step-3 Explore agent produces per project. It is the unit reviewed at the Step-4 gate and consumed by the Step-5 implementer. Being structured makes it diffable, resumable, and checkable against the deterministic discover/changelog output rather than trusted as prose.

dir.description: absolute path to the project dir

summary.description: one-paragraph human overview of the migration

risk.description: overall risk; high = multiple breaking changes or a large refactor

bumps.description: version bumps, one per @llui/* dep that is behind. Must reconcile with discover.mjs output.

from.description: pinned version, or the range for local-link

breakingFixes.description: concrete edits required by breaking changes between from and to. Each ties a consumer call site to the llui commit that broke it.

package.description: the @llui/* package whose change forces this

lluiCommit.description: short hash from changelog.mjs that introduced the change

change.description: what changed in llui (the breaking delta)

fix.description: what to do at this site

refactors.description: library-replacement refactors: hand-rolled code that an llui library already provides (FOLDED INTO the migration per the user's decision). Keep as separately-labeled commits so they're reviewable apart from the bumps.

replaces.description: what custom code is being removed, e.g. 'hand-rolled accordion'

withLibrary.description: the @llui/* export to use instead, e.g. '@llui/components accordion'

behaviorParity.description: how parity with the old behavior is confirmed (which test / manual check)

upstreamIssues.description: genuine bugs/gaps in llui itself surfaced while planning (Step 6). Not consumer problems. These get fixed on a branch in the llui repo, never worked around in the consumer.

blocksMigration.description: true if the consumer cannot complete until this is fixed + released

verify.description: the oracles that decide Step-5 success — the migration is done only when these go green. Commands are run in the project dir.

commands.description: ordered: install, then build/typecheck/test. The llui vite-plugin's compile-time lint errors run as part of build — a botched migration will not compile.

manualChecks.description: things only a human/agent can confirm (e.g. a screenshot of the replaced component still renders)
